signInInformation.title: Changes to Login Process

description.text:
We have merged the login process for EuroLeagueTV and EuroLeague ID – now a single account lets you access both, plus our Fantasy Challenge game.

**If you previously just had a EuroLeagueTV account, please use the forgot password function to activate your new Euroleague ID account.**

If you previously just had a Euroleague ID account, congratulations! You now also have access to a free tier of videos on EuroLeagueTV.\*

**If you are accessing our Fantasy Challenge via our partner's website, please be informed that our partners do not process, collect nor store any user data of participating users as stated in our Privacy Policy**

signInInformation.asteriskText: *EuroLeagueTV is available worldwide excluding Spain, France, Italy and Monaco.

accountDeletionInfo.text:
Before continuing to delete your EuroLeague ID account, please bear in mind the following:

If you play the Euroleague Fantasy Challenge, you may want to delete your team there first, as it will be more difficult to do that if your account no longer exists. You can do that at [euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account](http://euroleaguefantasy.euroleague.net/en/account).

club.info: LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne was one of the first major powerhouses in France as it claimed 15 championships between 1949 and 1981. It reached the Saporta Cup finals in 1983 and then the 1997 EuroLeague Final Four, but when ASVEL conquered the French League in the spring of 2002, that was the club's first domestic crown in 21 years. In 2009, ASVEL won the French League to secure its return to the following season’s EuroLeague. A major step forward came in 2014 when Tony Parker became club president. The 2019-20 campaign saw a commendable return to the EuroLeague, with nine home wins before the season prematurely ended due to the COVID-19 pandemic. With Parker’s brother, T.J., newly installed as head coach, ASVEL collected 13 EuroLeague regular-season wins along with the French League and Cup double in 2021. In the 2021-22 campaign, ASVEL successfully defended the French championship for the first time since 1972. ASVEL lifted the Leaders Cup in 2023, but the last two seasons were trophyless campaigns for the French giant. With EuroLeague legend Nando De Colo still under contract and head coach Pierric Poupet entering his second full season in charge, ASVEL will be looking to improve on finishing at the bottom in each of the last two EuroLeague seasons.

heroMedia.title: Paris overcomes ASVEL thanks to Justin Robinson’s late heroics
heroMedia.summary: A massive fourth quarter surge from Robinson powers Paris to a comeback win after a shaky first half

linkedIds.text: Paris Basketball v LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
“I think it’s a very valuable win, for many reasons,” said Francesco Tabellini, moments after his Paris Basketball team earned a 90-81 win over fellow French side LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne. “I’m super proud of my players and the way they picked it up.”  
The Italian head coach had plenty to be proud of in the second half, but it wasn’t a straightforward victory for the Parisians.

Last-placed ASVEL set the tone in the opening quarter, executing well to finish the opening period with a 6-point advantage. The visiting side kept its foot on the gas in the second, moving the ball slickly and controlling the glass to take a 9-point lead into the halftime break.

The hosts came back swinging in the second half, rallying back with a 7-0 run early in the third period to carve a route back into the contest. Though ASVEL offered some resistance, Paris poured in the points, eventually putting together a 29-point quarter and taking control of the game.

Pierric Poupet’s team fought back down the stretch, but the home team couldn’t be stopped. Paris guard Justin Robinson exploded for 12 points in the final 2 minutes of the game to propel his team over the line. When the final buzzer sounded, they had secured a 9-point victory.

The 28-year-old Robinson was the undeniable star of the show, adding 6 assists and 6 boards to a game-changing 25-point scoring performance. His back-to-back clutch threes in the fourth made a huge difference, but Robinson attributed the plays to the confidence given to him by his teammates.

“They were daggers,” said Robinson. “You can hear my teammates in the background, they give me confidence all the time. I started off slow, missed a lot of threes, but made them when it mattered. My coaches and teammates trusted me to step up big. I stepped up to the challenge and made big shots down the stretch.”

Having impressed during his first season in the French capital, Robinson seems to be enjoying every moment.

“This is who I am as a character and as a player,” Robinson shared. “I like to have fun. In those big moments down the stretch, I just try to get the crowd hyped, my teammates pumped up, and just keep going. It’s all fun and games, I’m blessed to play this game, in this beautiful league.”

Of course, Robinson couldn’t win the game alone. Allan Dokossi was a highly effective supporting act, recording 12 points and 5 rebounds and Lamar Stevens added 11 points. Overall, Tabellini was encouraged by the way his team performed in the end.

“We started the game pretty poorly, shooting-wise, and lost a bit of our trust. Then we kept our composure and didn’t allow them to take a big lead, so in the second half it was completely different, we put together great offense, it was amazing.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Paris Basketball 🏀 (@parisbasketball)

On the other bench, Poupet was understandably disappointed with the loss, but he sees it as yet another learning opportunity.

“We had a great first half, then a good 5 minutes at the end, but it wasn’t enough,” Poupet said. “We had a long stretch without scoring or making stops. We have to learn from it, we cannot play 25 good minutes, we have to make it 30, 35 or 40 maybe, so that we can win games.”

Poupet may hope for significant improvements, but ASVEL will now play for a combination of pride and future development. The loss moves his team to 8-23 for the season and mathematically eliminates it from playoff qualification.

Paris’s record improves to 12-19, as it holds onto an outside chance of postseason action. Tabellini’s men will face their next challenge on Tuesday, when they meet Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv.

linkedId.text: Paris Basketball v LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (2026-03-12T20:00:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.title: Paris overcomes ASVEL thanks to Justin Robinson’s late heroics
articleMetadata.description: Paris Basketball earned a 90–81 win over LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne behind a spectacular late surge from Justin Robinson, who tallied 25 points, 6 rebounds and 6 assists, including 12 points in the final two minutes. ASVEL controlled the first half, but Paris stormed back with a 29 point third quarter and strong supporting contributions from Allan Dokossi and Lamar Stevens. The victory improves Paris to 12–19 and preserves its slim postseason chances, while ASVEL falls to 8–23 and is eliminated from playoff contention.

heroMedia.title: Round 30 MVP: Braian Angola, LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne

content.text:
[Braian Angola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/braian-angola/010465/) delivered a dominant performance to lead LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne to just its second road win of the season, a stunning 88-91 victory at Anadolu Efes Istanbul. The 26-point outing earned Angola Round 30 MVP honors with a PIR of 28 – the highest among winning players this round – in just his seventh EuroLeague game.

Angola shot efficiently across the board: 4-of-7 two-pointers, 3-of-6 three-pointers and 9-of-11 free throws, while adding 4 rebounds, 2 assists and 9 fouls drawn. Angola earned his first-ever MVP of the Round award, becoming the second ASVEL player to secure this honor this season. Bastien Vautier was the Round 18 MVP with a PIR of 31, also against Efes.

[Saben Lee](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/saben-lee/013389/) of Efes posted the round's highest index rating with a 29 PIR, but it came in a losing effort against Angola’s ASVEL. Lee finished with 18 points on 5-of-7 two-pointers, 1-of-1 three-pointers and 5-of-5 free throws, adding 5 rebounds, 4 assists, 1 steal and 6 fouls drawn.

[Chima Moneke](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/chima-moneke/012201/) of Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade recorded a PIR of 28 in his team's 80-85 home loss to FC Bayern Munich. Moneke had 22 points on 7-of-8 two-pointers, 1-of-3 three-pointers and 5-of-7 free throws, in addition to 6 rebounds, 2 assists and 7 fouls drawn.

[Carsen Edwards](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/carsen-edwards/011987/) of Virtus Bologna and [Moses Wright](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/moses-wright/012878/) of Zalgiris Kaunas each posted a PIR of 27 to round out the top performances in Round 30. Edwards had 32 points in Virtus's 92-84 road loss to Real Madrid, hitting 7-of-10 two-pointers and 6-of-14 three-pointers, adding 3 rebounds, 5 assists, 1 steal and 4 fouls drawn. Wright had 20 points on 7-of-11 two-pointers and 6-of-7 free throws in Zalgiris's 91-87 road loss to Valencia Basket, with 9 rebounds, 1 assist and 5 fouls drawn. 

### Individual highs: Walter Tavares, Real Madrid, 5 blocks

Carsen Edwards led Round 30 in scoring with 32 points against Real Madrid. Nadir Hifi of Paris Basketball leads the overall standings with 19.5 points per game.

Alen Smailagic of Virtus and Alan Dokossi of Paris each pulled down a round-best 11 rebounds. Nikola Milutinov of Olympiacos Piraeus leads the competition with 7.3 rebounds per game.

Mike James of Monaco and Codi Miller-McIntyre of Crvena Zvezda each dished out a round-high 8 assists. Crvena Zvezda's Codi Miller-McIntyre leads the EuroLeague with 6.9 assists per game.

Mamadi Diakite of Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz collected 4 steals in Round 30. Aleksa Avramovic of Dubai Basketball leads the competition with 1.5 steals per game.

Walter Tavares of Real Madrid had 5 blocks against Virtus. He leads the EuroLeague with 2.0 blocks per game.

articleMetadata.title: Round 30 MVP: Braian Angola, LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne
articleMetadata.description: Braian Angola delivered a dominant performance to lead LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne to just its second road win of the season, a stunning 88-91 victory at Anadolu Efes Istanbul. The 26-point outing earned Angola Round 30 MVP honors with a PIR of 28 – the highest among winning players this round – in just his seventh EuroLeague game.

heroMedia.title: ASVEL 'has fun' and keeps Efes's number, wins 88-91 in Istanbul
heroMedia.summary: ASVEL has five straight wins against Efes

linkedIds.text: Anadolu Efes Istanbul v LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (2026-03-06T17:30:00.000Z)

content.text:
[LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/ldlc-asvel-villeurbanne/asv/) lost its first 11 road games this season but has now won two of its last four after holding off [Anadolu Efes Istanbul](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/anadolu-efes-istanbul/ist/) 88-91 on Friday night. This also marked ASVEL's fifth consecutive win against Efes, though it still couldn't lift the team from last place in the standings, where it sits with an 8-22 record.

"We don't win too many games, so I have to be satisfied. I can't be anything but satisfied after a game like this," ASVEL head coach Pierric Poupet said in a postgame EuroLeague TV interview.

content.title: Anadolu Efes Istanbul-LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne | Round 30 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
In a turnover-filled game with 35 combined miscues, ASVEL managed to survive a late charge by Efes led by [Sehmus Hazer](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/sehmus-hazer/011201/), who scored 8 of his career-high 20 points in the final 2:20. But ASVEL had [Braian Angola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/braian-angola/010465/), who finished with 26 points – a personal best in his seventh EuroLeague game.

"We came with an idea to play as a team, play together," Angola said in a postgame EuroLeague TV interview. "I think my teammates have a lot of trust in me and my quality, and my coach has the same. He always tells me to go out there and have fun. When you have that type of confidence from your coach and your teammates, it is always great to play basketball and have fun."

ASVEL opened up a 12-point lead in the first quarter behind [Zac Seljaas](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/zachary-seljaas/014088/) and [Armel Traore](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/armel-traore/010319/), but [Saben Lee](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/saben-lee/013389/) entered late in the first quarter and the deficit started melting. Three-pointers from Seljaas and Angola kept ASVEL ahead, but [Rolands Smits](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/rolands-smits/007109/) hit a pair of triples, and Lee continued to harass ASVEL's defense as the hosts went in front 42-40. However, in the final two minutes of the half, Heurtel hit a pair of three-pointers, including one with the final second, to make it 47-49 at halftime.

Les paniers et le sang froid qu’il faut sur la fin.

MVP Braian ⭐️ pic.twitter.com/YUw85u78zJ

— LDLC ASVEL (@LDLCASVEL) March 6, 2026

Apart from the opening minute, ASVEL remained ahead the entire second half, but had to navigate considerable drama. The third quarter saw the two teams combine for 12 turnovers. ASVEL used that to stay ahead and pull away further in the fourth quarter with a three from Angola to make it 65-73, then as Efes threatened to rally, back-to-back threes from [Glynn Watson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/glynn-watson/014013/) extended the lead to 76-83.

But Smits hit from long range once, then Hazer connected twice to get the hosts within 86-87. ASVEL didn't score a field goal in the remaining time, but [David Lighty](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/david-lighty/003526/) made a defensive play forcing Lee's offensive foul, and Angola was perfect from the foul line to preserve ASVEL's win.

"The plan is to keep fighting until the end, not just today, but all the way through the season. To see if we can leave somebody behind us," Poupet said.

The fight was also evident in ASVEL's 11-5 advantage in offensive rebounds. Though ASVEL closed in on Efes, the team will need more wins to escape the bottom of the standings. This victory, however, could prove important in that regard, as ASVEL now holds a season sweep of Efes, which dropped to 9-21.

linkedId.text: Anadolu Efes Istanbul v LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (2026-03-06T17:30:00.000Z)

articleMetadata.description: LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne lost its first 11 road games this season but has now won two of its last four after holding off Anadolu Efes Istanbul 88-91 on Friday night. This also marked ASVEL's fifth consecutive win against Efes, though it still couldn't lift the team from last place in the standings, where it sits with an 8-22 record.

heroMedia.title: Dubai overcomes cold shooting night to defeat ASVEL
heroMedia.summary: Three-pointers weren’t falling in Round 29, but Jurica Golemac’s team proved just as effective inside the arc

linkedIds.text: Dubai Basketball v LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (2026-02-26T17:00:00.000Z)

content.text:
[Dubai Basketball](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/dubai-basketball/dub/) may be enjoying its best stretch of the season. Even without Davis Bertans and Boogie Ellis, most of the core rotation is healthy. In Round 29, Dubai defeated [LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/ldlc-asvel-villeurbanne/asv/) 95-84 despite struggling from long range, extending its winning streak to four games. The victory lifted the team back above .500 at 15-14.

The hosts made just 5-of-18 three-point attempts (26.3%). Center [Mfiondu Kabengele](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mfiondu-kabengele/012796/) knocked down a pair of early threes – including one off the dribble to beat the shot clock – but over the first three quarters, the rest of the team combined to shoot just 1 for 9 (11.1%) from deep. Rather than forcing outside shots, Dubai attacked the rim and fed its big men in the paint – and the adjustment paid off. In the process, the team set a new club record.

content.title: Dubai Basketball-LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne | Round 29 Highlights | 2025-26 EuroLeague

content.text:
Dubai converted 34 two-point field goals, the most in club history. The previous mark was 30 in a 95-89 home win over Zalgiris Kaunas in Round 11. [Dwayne Bacon](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/dwayne-bacon/011286/) and [McKinley Wright](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mckinley-wright-iv/012606/) each made seven shots inside the arc, leading the way in the record-setting performance. Bacon delivered an all-around effort with 18 points, 5 rebounds, 3 assists, 1 steal and 6 fouls drawn for a PIR of 24, despite missing all three of his attempts from beyond the arc.

Wright was especially sharp in the first half, helping build a 48-36 lead at the break with 8 points, 3 rebounds, 6 assists and no turnovers. He finished with 16 points, 5 rebounds and 7 assists – again without committing a turnover – for a team-high PIR of 27. Dubai also matched two club records with a team PIR of 120 and 7 blocked shots, with [Justin Anderson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/justin-anderson/012795/) contributing 3 of them.

“All that matters is that we got the win. We’re competing for something bigger, so all the credit to my teammates and my coaching staff for putting me in a position to showcase tonight,” Wright said before addressing the fans. “We appreciate all the love and support. Just keep showing up – we’re going to keep trying to win games for you guys.”

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Dubai Basketball (@dubaibasketballclub)

For the most part, it was smooth sailing. Dubai led 75-58 at the end of the third quarter and only allowed ASVEL to chip away at the margin once the result was no longer in doubt. Afterward, coach Jurica Golemac praised his team’s performance over the decisive stretch.

“Congratulations to the players. We played a good game for, I would say, 35 minutes. In the last five minutes, we slowed down, but our energy was at the right level. We deserved to win,” Golemac said.

Dubai finished February undefeated and remains in the race for a Play-In spot, sitting 11th in the standings. As Bacon emphasized, the key is maintaining the same approach.

“That’s the nature of the game – to have fun. We’re in a great rhythm right now. A lot of guys did a lot of good things tonight, and we just want to keep this pace,” Bacon said. “The mindset is to fight and take it one game at a time. That’s what we’ve been doing all month. It’s been a pretty good month for us.”

articleMetadata.title: Dubai overcomes cold shooting night to defeat ASVEL
articleMetadata.description: Dubai Basketball may be enjoying its best stretch of the season. In Round 29, Dubai defeated LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne 95-84 despite struggling from long range, extending its winning streak to four games. The victory lifted the team back above .500 at 15-14.

heroMedia.title: Phase 2 general public tickets for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad sell out

content.text:
The extraordinary demand for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad shows no signs of slowing down, as all tickets made available during Phase 2 of public sales have officially sold out.

When Phase 2 opened on February 26 at 10:00 CET, fans once again responded in massive numbers. Following Phase 1, which saw more than one million fans connect to the ticketing platform and resulted in a complete sellout, the second allocation was also quickly exhausted, reaffirming the immense appeal of European basketball’s premier event.

The overwhelming global interest highlights the unique atmosphere and significance of the Final Four, with fans from across Europe and beyond eager to secure their place in Athens for the season’s ultimate showdown.

Fans who were unable to purchase tickets during General public tickets sale Phase 2 will still have an additional opportunity on February 26 as VIP tickets will be available starting at 14:00 CET.

Fans who were unable to secure tickets during Phase 1 will still have multiple opportunities to attend European basketball’s premier event. The second public ticket sales phase will open on February 26 at 10:00 CET. The third public ticket sales phase will open on March 17 at 10:00 CET, while the final sales phase will begin on April 20 at 10:00 CEST, coinciding with the conclusion of the Regular Season as teams continue their pursuit of a Final Four berth.

Each remaining phase will feature a limited allocation across multiple price categories, and high demand is expected once again.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens Presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

heroMedia.title: Power rankings: After the cups have gone
heroMedia.summary: Our experts offer up a suggestion for every team through 28 rounds

content.text:
Welcome to the latest edition of the EuroLeague Power Rankings. The list is the brainchild of [EuroLeague.net](http://EuroLeague.net)'s team of experts, which is made up of Geoff Gillingham, Javier Gancedo, Frankie Sachs and Igor Petrinovic. The rankings are not a prediction of what the final standings will look like, but rather where all 20 teams stand – in their humble opinions – today, after Round 28.

 

### 1. Fenerbahce Beko Istanbul (20-7)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Besiktas on Sunday to win the Turkish Cup

**The question we are asking:** Is Fenerbahce aware that not team to finish the regular season in first place under the current format has won the championship?

 

### 2. Olympiacos Piraeus (18-9)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Panathinaikos in the Greek Cup final on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Do we need to start worrying about the health of the Reds’ stars?

 

### 3. Real Madrid (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Baskonia in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can *Los Blancos* improve its defense to become a top contender?

### 4. Panathinaikos AKTOR Athens (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Olympiacos to win the Greek Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Will the addition of Nigel Hayes-Davis be enough to put the Greens over the top?

 

### 5. Valencia Basket (18-10)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** How will Valencia shake its disappointment from not winning the Spanish Cup at home? 

 

### 6. FC Barcelona (17-11)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Barcelona in the Spanish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** With Punter back in action, can Barça improve its consistency and stay in the top-six race?

 

### 7. Zalgiris Kaunas (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Rytas Vilnius in the Lithuanian Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** With six home games remaining and good momentum, is a top-six finish a realistic goal?

### 8. AS Monaco (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Le Mans to win the French Leaders Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After a turbulent stretch on and off the court, can this trophy galvanize Monaco and stabilize its EuroLeague push?

 

### 9. Hapoel IBI Tel Aviv (16-11)

**What they did last week:** Went 3-0 in the Israeli League

**The question we are asking:** Is Hapoel’s recent slide just a bump in the road or a sign that the team peaked too early?

 

### 10. Crvena Zvezda Meridianbet Belgrade (16-12)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Mega Belgrade to win the Serbian Cup on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Jordan Nwora the star who can carry Zvezda to new heights? 

 

### 11. Dubai Basketball (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bosna Sarajevo in the ABA League

**The question we are asking:** Now that Dzanan Musa is back and in shape, can Dubai win enough games to make it to the Play-In Showdown?

### 12. Maccabi Rapyd Tel Aviv (13-15)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Bnei Herzliya to win the Israeli Cup on Thursday

**The question we are asking:** Is there enough time left in the season for Maccabi to climb to 10th place?

 

### 13. EA7 Emporio Armani Milan (14-14)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Tortona to win the Italian Cup on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** Can Milan translate domestic momentum into EuroLeague consistency and revive its play-in hopes?

 

### 14. FC Bayern Munich (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bamberg in German Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What would this team have looked like with a full season under Coach Pesic?

 

### 15. Anadolu Efes Istanbul (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Besiktas in the Turkish Cup semifinal on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Is Efes destined to become a spoiler for a few teams in the playoff race?

### 16. Virtus Bologna (12-16)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Tortona in the Italian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** What adjustments can Dusko Ivanovic make to halt Virtus’s slide – especially away from home?

 

### 17. Partizan Mozzart Bet Belgrade (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Mega Belgrade in the Serbian Cup semifinals on Saturday

**The question we are asking:** Can Joan Penarroya realign the locker room and restore Partizan’s competitive edge?

 

### 18. Paris Basketball (9-18)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Bourg in the French Leader’s Cup quarterfinals on Friday

**The question we are asking:** What will it take for Paris to recapture its early-season identity and get Nadir Hifi back to his best?

 

### 19. Kosner Baskonia Vitoria-Gasteiz (9-19)

**What they did last week:** Defeated Real Madrid in the Spanish Cup final on Sunday

**The question we are asking:** After its Spanish Cup success and strong home performances, can Baskonia start winning consistently on the road?

 

### 20. LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne (7-21)

**What they did last week:** Lost to Monaco in the French Cup quarterfinals on Tuesday

**The question we are asking:** With the postseason out of reach, which players are auditioning for their EuroLeague futures?

heroMedia.title: 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad – Ticket sales Phase 2 to start on February 26
heroMedia.summary: Phase 1 general public ticket sale attracted over 1 million fans worldwide

content.text:
Ticket sales for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad will enter their next chapter on February 26 at 10:00 CET, as Phase 2 opens to the general public.

The launch of Phase 2 coincides with the closing of the EuroLeague players’ market, ensuring that fans will have a clear picture of each team’s final roster.

All fans,  except those who have already purchased tickets during previous sales phases or pre-sales , will be eligible to access the February 26 general public sale starting at 10:00 CET. During this second sales window,  all fans will also have the opportunity to purchase VIP tickets starting at 14:00 CET .

Ticketing information is available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/). Fans must log in or create a EuroLeague ID account to complete their purchase. Once logged in, all available categories will be displayed. Due to expected high demand and to ensure fairness, each fan may purchase a maximum of two tickets.

For the first time, Final Four tickets are being released in multiple sales phases. This approach is designed to expand access for the EuroLeague’s growing global fan base, offering multiple opportunities to secure tickets,  whether fans plan to attend regardless of the qualified teams or prefer to wait until the competition reaches its decisive stages. All phases follow a fair access policy, meaning that fans who purchase tickets in one phase will not be eligible to participate in subsequent phases. This measure is designed to ensure broader access to the event.

Phase 1, held on February 11, experienced massive demand, as over a million fans connected to sold out all available tickets in less than five minutes, once again demonstrating the enormous appeal of the EuroLeague’s premier event.

The 2026 EuroLeague Final Four semifinals will be played on Friday, May 22, at 18:00 and 21:00 local time (17:00 and 20:00 CEST). On Sunday, May 24, the adidas NextGen EuroLeague Championship Game will tip off at 18:00 local time (17:00 CEST), while the season will culminate with the Championship Game at 21:00 local time (20:00 CEST), when a new EuroLeague champion will be crowned.

A third ticket sales window will open on Tuesday, March 17, at 10:00 CET, offering another opportunity for fans to secure their seats. The final sales phase will launch on Monday, April 20, at 10:00 CEST, positioned between the conclusion of the Regular Season and the start of the Play-In Showdown, with 10 teams still in contention for a place in the Final Four.

All ticketing details for the 2026 EuroLeague Final Four Athens presented by Etihad are available at [https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/](https://euroleaguef4tickets.com/en/).

heroMedia.title: French Leaders Cup: Monaco, Bourg to the semis!
heroMedia.summary: Monaco and Bourg made it to the semifinals. They will  face Cholet and Le Mans, respectively.

content.text:
### Bourg holds off Paris's comeback

Cosea JL Bourg-en-Bresse got closer to the French Leaders Cup title by edging Paris Basketball 74-78 in the last quarterfinals showdown at Arena Futuroscope. [Ricky Lindo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/ricardo-lindo/013902/) led the winners with 16 points and 10 rebounds. [Kevin Kokila](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/kevin-kokila/011837/) added 13 points and nine rebounds, [Will McDowell-White](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/will-mcdowell-white/008062/) had 11 points while [Darius McGhee](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/darius-mcghee/013859/) and Asseman Moulare each got 10 for Bourg. McGhee added 10 assists for a double-double. [Nadir Hifi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nadir-hifi/012640/) led Paris with 17 points while [Amath M'Baye](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/amath-m-baye/007993/) added 13. 

[Justin Robinson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/justin-robinson/014008/) and [Yakuba Ouattara](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/yakuba-ouattara/004197/) each struck from downtown to give Paris an early 10-5 lead. [Adam Mokoka](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/adam-mokoka/012698/) joined the three-point shootout to put Bourg in charge, 14-16, soon after that. Hifi and [Allan Dokossi](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/allan-dokossi/014010/) helped Paris tie the game, 23-23, at the end of the first quarter. [Momo Faye](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/mouhamed-faye/012403/) scored down low to give Paris its last lead, 33-31, as Kokila and Mokoka soon restored a 33-38 Bourg edge. Lindo buried a triple that boosted his team's lead to 40-47 at halftime. 

Lindo kept pacing Bourg after the break, getting help from [Both Gach](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/both-gach/013900/) and Mokoka to make it a double-digit game, 47-58. M'Baye and Hifi brought Paris within 56-60 at the end of the third quarter. M'Baye buried a three-pointer to get his team even closer, 61-62, with 6 minutes left. [Tajuan Agee](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/eurocup/players/tajuan-agee/014182/) and Moulare rescued Bourg before Lindo's driving layup gave his team fresh air at 63-70. Lamar Stevens got Paris within striking distance, 74-77, already in the final minute. Hifi missed from beyond the arc, and Mokoka sealed the outcome from the foul line.

𝐃𝐈𝐑𝐄𝐂𝐓𝐈𝐎𝐍 𝐋𝐄𝐒 𝐃𝐄𝐌𝐈-𝐅𝐈𝐍𝐀𝐋𝐄𝐒 🔥@ParisBasketball 74 🆚 78 JL Bourg

Job is done. Rendez-vous demain 😤@Betclic_ELITE pic.twitter.com/paYpLcBkIG

— JL Bourg Basket (@JLBourgBasket) February 20, 2026

#### Monaco rallies past Strasbourg

AS Monaco reached the semifinals by rallying from a 16-point deficit to beat Strasbourg 90-89 at Arena Futuroscope. [Nikola Mirotic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/nikola-mirotic/000796/) led the winners with 17 points. [Elie Okobo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/elie-okobo/011212/) added 15, [Daniel Theis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/daniel-theis/004132/) and [Matthew Strazel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/matthew-strazel/008099/) each had 11, while [Terry Tarpey](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/terry-tarpey/012515/) and [Juhann Begarin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/juhann-begarin/008161/) got 10 apiece for Monaco. 

Gabe Brown and Marcus Keene each had 6 points to help Strasbourg build an early double-digit lead, 4-16. Keene soon boosted his team's margin to 11-27. Begarin and Okobo stepped up for Monaco, combining for 10 points in a 14-0 run that brought their team within 25-27. Mirotic took over with a three-point play that completed Monaco's comeback, 32-29. Okobo's three-pointer capped a mammoth 33-2 run and gave Monaco a 15-point lead, 44-29, which Strasbourg trimmed to 51-42 at halftime. 

Mirotic took over with a four-point play, starting a 14-3 run that Strazel capped with free throws for a 69-54 Monaco margin. Monaco kept a 75-63 lead after 30 minutes before Strasbourg erased half of its deficit for an 83-78 score with over 4 minutes left. Brown's three-point play completed the guests' comeback, 84-86. Mirotic and Theis allowed Monaco to tie it at 89-89 already in the final minute. Mirotic split free throws, and Keene missed at the other end.

🎩🤌 AAAND ONE!!#LeadersCup #RocaTeam #DagheMunegu pic.twitter.com/mcMjxA8sNX

— AS Monaco Basket 🇲🇨 (@ASMonaco_Basket) February 20, 2026

### Cholet beats ASVEL in tournament opener

 

The French Leaders Cup is underway at Arena Futuroscope! Cholet Basket was the first team to make it to the semifinals. Three more games will be played today. 

LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne registered an 82-93 loss against Cholet in the opening game of the tournament. DeAndre Gholston led Cholet with 28 points. [Thomas Heurtel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/thomas-heurtel/klt/) led ASVEL with 20 points and seven assists. [Glynn Watson Jr](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/glynn-watson/014013/). added 19 points, [Bastien Vautier](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/bastien-vautier/014014/) had 13 while [Melvin Ajinca](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/melvin-ajinca/010316/) got 11 for ASVEL. 

Ajinca hit back-to-back triples, and Vautier dunked twice in a 13-0 run which allowed ASVEL to get a double-digit lead, 28-16, at the end of the first quarter. [Edwin Jackson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/players/edwin-jackson/000229/) and Heurtel each joined the three-point shootout to boost ASVEL's margin to 42-25. Cholet rallied to take a 65-67 lead right at the start of the fourth quarter, extending it to 79-81 to seal the outcome in the next-to-last minute. 

Monaco and Cholet will go against each other in the first semifinal at 18:30 on Saturday. Later on today, it's Nanterre vs. Le Mans and Paris Basketball vs. Cosea JL Bourg-en-Bresse to complete the second semifinal.

articleMetadata.title: French Leaders Cup: four games tonight!
articleMetadata.description:
The French Leaders Cup is underway at Arena Futuroscope! Cholet Basket was the first team to make it to the semifinals. Three more games will be played today. 

LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne registered a 82-93 loss against Cholet in the opening game of the tournament. DeAndre Gholston led Cholet with 28 points. Thomas Heurtel led ASVEL with 20 points and seven assists. Glynn Watson Jr. added 19 points, Bastien Vautier had 13 while Melvin Ajinca got 11 for ASVEL.

heroMedia.title: French Cup: Monaco sneaks past ASVEL into the semis
heroMedia.summary: The two teams will now turn their attention to the Leaders Cup, which gets underway on Friday

content.text:
[AS Monaco](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/teams/as-monaco/mco/) withstood a furious late rally to edge [LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/en/euroleague/teams/ldlc-asvel-villeurbanne/asv/) 95-98 on Tuesday night at the Astroballe, punching its ticket to the French Cup semifinals. [Elie Okobo](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/elie-okobo/011212/) scored 22 points for the winners, with [Daniel Theis](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/daniel-theis/004132/) adding 19.

Playing without injured forwards Alpha Diallo and Kevarrius Hayes – and with Yoan Makoundou – Monaco relied on balance and timely shot-making to knock out one of its biggest domestic rivals. Five players scored at least 8 points as the Roca Team advanced to the last four and stayed in contention on all fronts this season.

ASVEL’s [Glynn Watson](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/glynn-watson/014013/) led all scorers with 31 points while [Braian Angola](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/braian-angola/010465/) netted 22, but the hosts couldn’t overcome Monaco’s efficiency and late-game execution.

🙌🇲🇨 La #RocaTeam en demi-finale de la Coupe de France ! 🏆#DagheMunegu #CDFBasket pic.twitter.com/eP6clvL46c

— AS Monaco Basket 🇲🇨 (@ASMonaco_Basket) February 17, 2026

Monaco set the tone early behind Theis, who scored his team’s first five points and established a presence inside. [Mike James](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/mike-james/005985/) mixed scoring and playmaking, and Okobo capped the opening quarter with a deep buzzer-beater to give Monaco an 18-22 lead. ASVEL kept pace behind its work on the offensive glass and transition opportunities. The teams traded leads in the second quarter before Monaco closed the half strong. Theis dominated the interior and had 15 points at the break, helping the visitors carry a 42-46 advantage into halftime.

Monaco appeared to seize control early in the third. [Nemanja Nedovic](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/nemanja-nedovic/000848/) knocked down a three-pointer and added a transition basket to stretch the lead to 53–43. But ASVEL answered with its best stretch of the night. [Melvin Ajinca](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/melvin-ajinca/010316/) converted a three-point play and hit another triple, igniting a 13–0 run. Watson followed with free throws to put the hosts back in front. Monaco steadied itself at the line and regained momentum late in the quarter. [Juhann Begarin](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/juhann-begarin/008161/) and [Terry Tarpey](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/terry-tarpey/012515/) provided a lift off the bench as Monaco closed on a strong note to take a 65-71 lead into the fourth.

[Jaron Blossomgame](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/jaron-blossomgame/011231/) opened the final period with a three-pointer, and Okobo followed with a tough finish to push the lead to nine. Tarpey later connected from beyond the arc to extend the cushion to 70-81. ASVEL refused to go away, trimming the deficit to six with under five minutes to play. That’s when [Matthew Strazel](https://www.euroleaguebasketball.net/euroleague/players/matthew-strazel/008099/) delivered the biggest sequence of the night – a contested jumper followed by a driving layup – to halt the momentum. Okobo’s three-point play with 3:30 remaining made it 78-88 and gave Monaco breathing room. ASVEL made it interesting in the final minute, but Monaco converted enough free throws to seal the 95-98 win.

Monaco and ASVEL now turn their attention to the Leaders Cup, which gets underway on Friday.

articleMetadata.title: French Cup: Monaco sneaks past ASVEL into the semis
articleMetadata.description: AS Monaco withstood a furious late rally to edge LDLC ASVEL Villeurbanne 95-98 on Tuesday night at the Astroballe, punching its ticket to the French Cup semifinals. Elie Okobo scored 22 points for the winners, with Daniel Theis adding 19.
